behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive/subdirectory event delivery). MOCK-MISS and REAL-INFRA both apply: this can fail only when daemon interacts with OS file events. Rejected higher levels because behavior text contains no AI/ML step ("file ... subdirectory ... processed") and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temporary directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is explicitly about deduplication logic for same path + time window; this is local state/time-window logic and can fail within one module without I/O. Rejected Integration because no required cross-module handoff is stated beyond "deduplication logic". Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no OS/network dependency, no AI/ML output, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component directly: submit path P at t0 (accepted), submit P again at t0+delta within window (rejected), and at t0+window+epsilon (accepted). Include config override for window length.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test remains inside one object/function boundary with no filesystem watcher or subprocess.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events; this is a real infrastructure boundary. In-process mocks can hide coalescing/burst patterns, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is pure filesystem/event handling with no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real temp directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple fs events (e.g., write/rename or chunked write), capture processing logs/output, and assert exactly one pipeline execution for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real OS watcher invalidation/rebind after directory deletion/recreation. Integration fakes usually miss kernel watcher lifecycle edge cases. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is watcher resiliency only, no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon watching a real temp directory, delete that directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering key is filesystem creation timestamps under concurrent arrivals; real metadata resolution/event timing can break ordering and is an OS interaction boundary. Integration could catch queue-sort logic, but may miss real timestamp granularity/tie behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text concerns file ordering only.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create several files nearly simultaneously, record observed processing order, compare against actual filesystem creation timestamps used by daemon, and assert order matches policy.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System is required because test asserts behavior against real filesystem timestamps, not synthetic fixtures.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at real filesystem event/write-completion boundary (partial writes, close semantics, timing). Mocks often return fully written content immediately, hiding this class of bug. Rejected Agentic/Workflow since no AI/ML dependency and no agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, open a file for incremental/chunked writes in watched directory, trigger arrival event before final close, and verify processing starts only after write completion (e.g., post-close or stabilization window) and reads full content.
